case_title: LAURA KATHLEEN BEATY-FALCON VS. MAY TOM et al

docket_entries.description: TRIAL RESET TO MAY-22-2013 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506 TRIAL OF APR-03-2013 IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF MAY-22-2013 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF MAY-22-2013 BY DONALD S. MITCHELL APPEARANCES BY BOTH PARTIES. PLAINTIFF APPEARED TELEPHONICALLY THROUGH COURT CALL. PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED BY MAIL RETAINED. JUDGE: DONALD S. MITCHELL. CLERK: M. GOODMAN. NOT REPORTED. (506)

"DEFENDANT'S EXPLANATION FOR NOT TIMELY PROVIDING AN ACCOUNTING OR RETURNING PLAINTIFF'S SECURITY DEPOSIT OF $3,600.00 IS NOT CREDIBLE. PLAINTIFF PROVIDED DEFENDANT WITH HER TEMPORARY AND PERMANENT ADDRESSES AND HER CELL PHONE NUMBER. PLAINTIFF ALSO TELEPHONED DEFENDANT REPEATEDLY AND LEFT MESSAGES WITH NO RESPONSE. NONETHELESS, DEFENDANT TESTIFIED THAT HE MAILED THREE LETTERS (DATED SEPTEMBER 4,11 AND 28, 2012) TO PLAINTIFF AT THE TENANCY ADDRESS AFTER PLAINTIFF VACATD THE TENANCY. NONE OF THE LETTERS WERE RECEIVED BY THE PLAINTIFF NOR WERE THEY RETURNED TO THE DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT USED AN INCORRECT ZIP CODE (WHICH HE DESCRIBED AS "TYPOS")ON ALL THREE LETTERS. DEFENDANT'S TESTIMONY CONCERNING THE THREE LETTERS IS NOT CREDIBLE. DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT TOWARD PLAINTIFF WAS ESPECIALLY EGREGIOUS;NOT ONLY DID DEFENDANT STONEWALL PLAINTIFF FOR MONTHS, KNOWING SHE HAD MOVED TO WYOMING AND FORCING HER TO FILE THIS ACTION..." PER JUDGE MITCHELL (FOR JUDGMENT AT LENGTH, SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF MAY-22-2013 - JUDGMENT.

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-22-2013 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES FOR THURSDAY, AUG-22-2013: ANDRES SANCHEZ, ESQ. APPEARS FOR APPELLANT MS. MAY TOM, WHO IS ALSO PRESENT THIS DAY; RESPONDENT MS. LAURA KATHLEEN BEATY-FALCON IS ALSO PRESENT IN THE COURTROOM. TRIAL DE NOVO HELD; THE COURT HEARS TESTIMONY PRESENTED BY BOTH SIDES AND ACCEPTS, READS AND REVIEWS ALL DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE WHICH IS IDENTIFIED, MARKED AND SUBMITTED TO THE COURT.

AFTER HEARING ALL TESTIMONY PRESENTED BY BOTH SIDES, AND AFTER RECEIVING ALL EVIDENCE GIVEN TO THE COURT, THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. ALL EXHIBITS TO BE RETURNED TO RESPECTIVE COUNSEL AND PARTIES VIA U.S. MAIL.

JUDGE: HON A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK MARJORIE L. MANZELLA; NOT REPORTED (D-502)
